channel.title: hardware on ecmccfg
channel.description: Recent content in hardware on ecmccfg

item.title: Diagnostics
item.description: Scope Use this page when a Beckhoff drive or encoder terminal exposes diagnostic messages that need to be read directly from the slave. Diagnostics The more advanced Beckhoff EtherCAT slaves, like drives and encoder readers, have a diagnostics buffer that can be read with the ec_diagnostic_messages.py tool: Example: EL7211-9014 python3 ec_diagnostic_messages.py -m3 -s14 DEVICE INFORMATION: =================== name: EL7211-9014 master id: 3 slave id: 14 vendor id: 0x2 product id: 0x1c2b3052 host time: 2026-02-24 15:39:38.

item.title: Hardware issues
item.description: Scope Use this page as a short log of known recurring hardware issues seen in real systems. Hardware Issues List of hardware issues at PSI: Date Type Description 03.06.25 Cable OCT cable failure. Motor enable was lost frequently; error bit on drives was set. Related Pages hardware

item.title: ELxxxx
item.description: Scope Use this page for general Beckhoff terminal power-bus questions, especially mixed 5V and 24V terminal chains. Power bus - 5V or 24V terminals 24V is the most common signal level for the terminals. However, some also support other voltage levels, for instance 5V or 12V. In that case, normally the terminal needs to be powered with the same voltage. For 5V terminals, normally an EL9505 is added before the 5V terminal in the EtherCAT chain, supplying the power bus with 5V.

item.title: EL1xxx
item.description: Scope Use this page for EL1xxx digital-input terminals with special timing or signal level behavior. EL1252, EL1252-0050 EL1252-xxxx is a two-channel digital-input terminal with timestamps for both low-to-high and high-to-low edges: EL1252: 24V signals EL1252-0050: 5V signals Both terminals have the same product ID and process data and can therefore be configured as EL1252: # One channel: ${SCRIPTEXEC} ${ecmccfg_DIR}addSlave.cmd, "SLAVE_ID=16, HW_DESC=EL1252" These terminals are useful because they can latch the timestamp of the positive edge and/or negative edge of an input signal independently of the EtherCAT bus rate.

item.title: EL5042
item.description: Scope Use this page for EL5042 BiSS-C/SSI encoder-terminal issues such as ready/error state, communication quality, supply voltage, clock frequency, and bit-count configuration. Topics Error, warning, not ready Diagnostics Offset LSB Bit [Bit] (0x80p8:17) SSI 9V supply RLS/Renishaw LA11 encoder Error, warning, not ready Could be caused by: Wrong settings (bit counts, etc.); see further below on this page (and also motion/best practice). Bad electrical connection Wrong power supply Defective encoder or EL5042 Long cabling lengths Wrong communication frequency Always start troubleshooting by checking the error, warning, and ready bits and reading the EL5042 manual.

item.title: EL51xx
item.description: Scope Use this page for EL51xx incremental-encoder terminals, especially slave selection, channel mode, oversampling, and trigger/cam-capable variants. Overview The EL51xx series covers incremental encoder interfaces: EL5101: 1 ch, diff rs422, ttl, 1MHz EL5101-0010: 1 ch, diff rs422, 5MHz EL5101-0011: 1 ch, diff rs422, 5MHz, oversampling 100kHz EL5102: 2ch diff rs422, 5MHz, PSI standard EL5112: 2 ch ABC or 1 ch AB, rs422, 5MHz EL5131: 1 ch, diff rs422, 5MHz, 2 digital outputs for cam/trigger Diagnostics General Normally, incremental encoder interfaces do not require any SDO configuration.

item.title: EL/EP70x1
item.description: Scope Use this page for Beckhoff EL/EP70x1 stepper-drive issues such as warnings, motor-power problems, current-loop tuning, and speed-range questions. Topics Error/warning Tuning Speed range EP7041-3102 scaling Diagnostics Error/warning If the drive is in error/warning state and cannot be enabled, see drive refuses to enable. The most common reason for this error is: motor is not connected (PSI specific): motor cable in wrong connector (at PSI motor and limits have the same 5pin M12 connector) drive has no motor voltage (PSI specific): EL9227-5500 feeding the I/O bus is not initiated (at first power on the switches on the terminal needs to be engaged) Further information about the error/warning can be read from the drive’s diagnostic register, Index A010 STM Diag data Ch.

item.title: EL7062
item.description: Scope Use this page for EL7062-specific behavior such as warnings, open-loop counter issues, autotuning, and touch-probe/index homing setup. Topics Error/warning Open-loop counter jump Tuning Home on index pulse of incremental encoder Diagnostics Error/warning If the drive is in error/warning state and cannot be enabled, see drive refuses to enable. The most common reason for this error is: motor is not connected (PSI specific): motor cable in wrong connector (at PSI motor and limits have the same 5pin M12 connector) drive has no motor voltage (PSI specific): EL9227-5500 feeding the I/O bus is not initiated (at first power on the switches on the terminal needs to be engaged) Open-loop counter jump The EL7062 has a firmware bug if configured to run in open loop CSV.

item.title: EL72xx
item.description: Scope Use this page for EL72xx servo-drive issues such as diagnostics, warning/fault states, tuning, and installation-related OCT problems. Topics Diagnostics Error/warning Tuning Electrical installation issues OCT cable failure Diagnostics The diagnostics buffer can be read with the ec_diagnostic_messages.py tool: Example: EL7211-9014 python3 ec_diagnostic_messages.py -m1 -s3 DEVICE INFORMATION: =================== name: EL7211-9014 master id: 1 slave id: 3 vendor id: 0x2 product id: 0x1c2b3052 host time: 2026-02-05 11:33:41.191176 DIAGNOSTIC MESSAGES: ==================== msg_no time text_id text flags diag_code dynamic 1 2000-01-01 00:00:00 0x4411 0x1 0x1c21e000 0x0 2 2026-02-05 10:30:39.

item.title: EL9xxx
item.description: Scope Use this page for EL9xxx supply and distribution terminals, especially over-current protection modules and passive-terminal-related slave-index confusion. Topics Over-current protection Passive terminals Over-current protection In the standard setup at PSI, over-current protection modules are used to feed 24V to both the EtherCAT communication bus (E-bus) and the power bus of the EtherCAT slaves. If the over-current protection is not enabled, then the EtherCAT slaves will not receive power.

item.title: Technosoft iPOS4808, iPOS8020
item.description: Scope Use this page for Technosoft iPOS drive setup, especially FoE-based base configuration and the PSI-supported starter configurations. iPOSXXXX Main use cases for Technosoft drives at PSI are applications requiring: high currents STO (Safe Torque Off) DC motors Configuration Technosoft drives are powerful, but they are not especially simple to integrate. Many settings are not exposed as normal CoE SDOs, so a base configuration needs to be created in one of the Technosoft tools and downloaded before the drive is used for the first time.
